book_claims.claim: Recommended unsweetened plant milk for the Longevity Diet. Fortified coconut milk is a top source of calcium (451 mg per cup, 45% DV) and vitamin D (101 IU per cup, 25% DV). Recommended serving: 1 glass (240 mL); choose unsweetened; calcium- and vitamin-D-fortified versions preferred.
book_claims.mechanism: Plant-based calcium and vitamin D source when fortified; supports bone mineral density and vitamin D status without the saturated fat and IGF-1-raising effects of cow's milk dairy.

supplementary_research.finding: Fortified plant milks (including coconut milk) provide bioavailable calcium and vitamin D comparable to cow's milk, supporting bone health outcomes relevant to fall prevention and fracture risk reduction in aging populations.

Plant-based milk alternatives fortified with calcium and vitamin D represent viable substitutes for dairy in populations seeking to reduce IGF-1 and saturated fat intake while maintaining adequate bone-protective micronutrient status.

supplementary_research.source: Tan & Bhanu, Journal of Nutritional Science (2023) — PMID 36789264
supplementary_research.finding: Coconut milk medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs) have a distinct metabolic fate compared to long-chain fatty acids, with rapid hepatic oxidation potentially reducing hepatic fat accumulation; however, total saturated fat content warrants moderation in individuals with cardiovascular risk.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Bioavailability of calcium from fortified plant milks is comparable to cow's milk (~30–35% absorption); best absorbed when vitamin D is co-present.
Fat-soluble; the coconut milk lipid matrix enhances absorption; critical for calcium metabolism, immune function, and muscle strength.
Rapidly absorbed and oxidized in liver; lower tendency to be stored as body fat compared to long-chain fatty acids; provide quick energy.

synergies.description: Coconut milk fat dramatically increases the bioavailability of curcumin from turmeric; the classic golden milk combination is nutritionally well-founded.
Coconut milk provides calcium and vitamin D to complement oat beta-glucan's cholesterol-lowering action, creating a breakfast combining cardiovascular and bone-health benefits.
